headlines.title: Fears of All Out War Grow as US-Iran Strikes Continue to Intensify
headlines.description: Concerns of a return to an all-out war are growing as the United States and Iran trade a new round of attacks in a second day of strikes. It comes as President Donald Trump says he would "bomb the s---- out of them" unless Iran signs a deal. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Defense industry leaders to meet with Trump amid missile supply worries, sources say
headlines.description: The president is expected to press the leaders to find ways to swiftly increase production of weaponry for the Pentagon, sources told NBC News.

headlines.title: They came for the World Cup. They fell in love with Buc-ee’s and ranch dip.
headlines.description: International tourists traveling through the U.S. ahead of FIFA’s festivities have been documenting their American experiences on social media.

headlines.title: Referee barred from entering U.S. for World Cup gets a hero’s welcome in Somalia
headlines.description: Somalia isn’t competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport, you’d think the country had won the trophy.

headlines.title: Workers at L.A.-area World Cup stadium reach tentative agreement after authorizing strike
headlines.description: Unite Here Local 11, which represents nearly 2,000 cooks, dishwashers, bartenders and concession workers at SoFi Stadium, voted just last week to authorize a strike.

headlines.title: Aging but still hungry: The legendary World Cup stars to watch
headlines.description: The World Cup, beginning Thursday afternoon, features the biggest names in global soccer, including two — Lionel Messi of Argentina and Cristiano Ronaldo — who are considered among the greatest to ever step on a field.

headlines.title: Hazmat crews respond to ‘hazardous materials incident’ at the Pentagon
headlines.description: Virginia emergency crews deployed to the Pentagon on Thursday morning for what authorities described as a “hazardous materials incident.”Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell confirmed that “sophisticated systems” had “detected an air quality issue necessitating precautionary measures until we determine its significance.”“The Department is executing standard protection protocols, including a shelter-in-place order for the affected area,” Parnell added, referring to the Defense Departme

headlines.title: How the Knicks pulled off the greatest comeback in NBA Finals history to move one win from glory
headlines.description: The New York Knicks overcame a 29-point deficit against the San Antonio Spurs in Game 4 to take a 3-1 lead in the NBA Finals. Here’s how it happened.

headlines.title: The inflation surge is hitting businesses, too
headlines.description: Wholesale inflation surged to its highest level since late 2022, as fuel prices pushed higher by the war with Iran ripple across the economy.

headlines.title: Elon Musk under fire for stoking anti-immigrant riots in Belfast
headlines.description: LONDON — The condemnation over anti-immigrant riots in Northern Ireland was being matched by another growing outrage in Britain on Thursday: that the world’s richest person was inciting the violence

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

headlines.title: Hazmat crews respond to ‘hazardous materials incident’ at the Pentagon
headlines.description: Virginia emergency crews deployed to the Pentagon on Thursday morning for what authorities described as a “hazardous materials incident.”Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell confirmed that “sophisticated systems” had “detected an air quality issue necessitating precautionary measures until we determine its significance.”“The Department is executing standard protection protocols, including a shelter-in-place order for the affected area,” Parnell added, referring to the Defense Departme
